title: 8-sample real bridge evaluation baseline

checkpoint.source: H0-003 remote run artifact for LIP-DATA-001

input_bundle.extraction_notes: Real hidden-state extraction; raw prompts and model text outputs are not stored in shards.

interpretation: The 8-sample run shows stronger global latent-space alignment than the 2-sample baseline: lower MSE/RMSE, higher matching cosine, better norm ratio, and lower energy drift.
Retrieval metrics do not yet show pair-level discrimination. retrieval_top1=0.125 equals chance for 8 candidates; retrieval_top5=0.625 equals chance for top-5 among 8 candidates.
cosine_diag_mean and offdiag_cosine_mean are nearly identical, indicating that predictions are entering a shared target-space region but not separating the correct target from incorrect targets.
diagonal_margin_mean is negative, indicating that the correct target is often not more similar than the strongest incorrect target.
The likely cause is undertraining: the H0-003 workflow used max_steps=2, so the model saw only two mini-batches.
The next technical step should be a configurable remote training workflow with larger max_steps/epochs and possibly stronger norm/MSE calibration.
known_limitations: The evaluation uses eight real latent pairs, which is larger than LIP-EVAL-002 but still tiny.
The remote training run completed only two CPU training steps.
Retrieval top-1 and top-5 match random chance for this candidate count.
The evaluation is latent-space only and does not evaluate text-level semantic fidelity.
The result does not establish model-to-model alignment, generalization, or production readiness.
data_policy: No generated eval outputs, checkpoints, shards, bundles, datasets, zips, caches, or run artifacts are committed.
scientific_claim_status: This PR registers a latent-space bridge evaluation baseline for an 8-sample real bundle. It does not claim semantic transfer, text-level fidelity, model-to-model alignment, generalization, or production readiness.
